reason:
These are distinct test cases in a test table, not duplicate code. Each has different semantic intent:
- "last page" tests pagination at the end (page 3 of 3, no next page)
- "exact page boundary" tests first page behavior (page 1 of 2, no previous page)

The similar structure is expected test table pattern with intentionally different values for different scenarios.

Different test scenarios with different semantics:

Both use the same boilerplate pattern (expectEmptyResults helper) but test fundamentally different behaviors. The similar structure is expected Ginkgo test pattern.
